Alright, you're back for part two of my forest gump pattern. We'll make you great strides here. So let's just get some context back. Now remember, the first half of the forest gump pattern was essentially setting the prospect straight in terms of what was really this objection about. You know, I want to think about it, call you back. It's really a smoke screen for uncertainty. We know that, right? So what we did here is by using the front half of the forest gump pattern, we got the client to say, yeah, well, actually it's the prospect. It's about to become a client, right? I'm going to catch myself, right? We got the prospect to say, yeah, well, then I would. Meaning, yeah, if I really knew you, if you had a track record, of course, then I'd be buying, right? And that's kind of logical because think about it now. He already said, oh, yeah, I love the product. That was what the first pattern was about. And then since he said that, and we use that sort of spacing in the mystery and entry tone, he hasn't really linked these two together yet. But he said, yeah, yeah, I love the product. He goes, yeah, well, I knew you, then I'd buy. And that's a very empowering place for the prospect to be. Except now what you're going to do is you're going to address the second 10 using the second half of the forest gump pattern, which remember from the buses, let me take a moment to reintroduce myself. Yes, if I knew you, I would do it. I say, now that I can understand. See the difference in my tone, now that I can understand. You don't know me. I don't have the luxury of a track record dot, dot, dot. So what happens now? So now I was like, yeah, and let me take a moment to reintroduce myself. And I've been with the company for seven years, three or whatever the number of months he is. And I pride myself on dot, dot, dot. Now here's the deal. Remember a forest gum pattern. It has to be personal to you. I can't say it for you. It has to consist of everything that you represent. In other words, you're signing any degrees you might have, any licenses you've attained, any special talent you have, any awards you want, even inside your company if they're private, what your goals are in general, what your goals are at the company, what you stand for as a human being in terms of your ethics and integrity and customer service. That's what you're doing here is you're telling the customer why they should not only trust you, but why this is even past your head. Of course, you could trust me. This here's who I am. Do you ask for the order right now? Yes or no? Of course not, right? You probably know better right now. You wouldn't do it because what? We still have the third 10 to address. No, you would not ask for the order. I hope you would have gotten this, but it's okay. You will not ask for the order yet because while you've addressed the first and second 10, we still have the third 10 to address. And this third 10 is what about the company that stands behind the product? And what you're going to do now with this forest gum pattern almost comes in two parts itself. The second part's almost two parts. You're going to move directly from your forest gum pattern to this new pattern, so to speak, but you're not going to have any pauses in between. You're not going to ask them any more questions. What you're going to do is very simple. You're going to add on some very simple words. There's actually seven words in all that you're going to add on to the end of your forest gum pattern that's going to allow you to transition directly into this new pattern, which is designed to sell the company. So let me tell you exactly what those magic words are. They're really simple. And as far as my company goes, that's it. You get it. In other words, let me show you how this would play out. So let's say the last point that you made in your forest gum pattern when you're talking to Billy, when you use Bill, right? In other words, I went out and wrote this incredible forest gum pattern, right? Three of them. Well, guess what? And you're going to spend as much time as it takes to write out the best possible version of your company on both a logical and an emotional basis. Now, in terms of specifics, and I'll go through this in the exercises as well, you're going to be saying things like, you know, where the number one this, me, my company's the number one this in the market, where the fastest growing that, where the foremost experts, where the most well-respected, company CEO, and they have a name of so-and-so, is one of the most astute minds in the industry. And that could be XYZ, whatever it is, right? He's accomplished X, the company's accomplished Y, and he's built this company out of one thing above all, whatever your mission or vision is, you get it? That's your third 10. So you're going to follow the same protocol that you did for the second 10 now with the third 10. And again, we'll lay this out, civil platitude, in the exercise section and in scripting. You have them both, right? Now, here's the deal. When you've completed this stirv pattern, now you've raised your prospects level of 34, each of the three 10s, it is now time to transition into your close, which you're going to do by saying something along the lines of, so Bill, let's do this, or why don't we do this? And then you're going to directly ask for the order now for a second time. Now, here's an important point. In addition, if the nature of your product, what you're selling, allows you to ask him for a smaller order, meaning to dip his toe in the water the first time around, okay? That will definitely increase your conversion. In other words, let's say you're selling stock, but using stock, it'll give you anything, any program, right? And you're recommending he buy 10,000 shares, you would now say, Bill, instead of buying 10,000 shares, why don't you start with 5,000 shares instead? You actually step down the amount, letting Bill dip his toe in the water, so to speak, to dip his toe in water first. And then you say, you know, if you see what a great job I can do for you, we can then work much bigger and better down the road. Does that sound fair enough? You get it? Now, let me explain this in more detail. We call this a step down sale. That's what this is, a step down sale. The way a step down sale works is as follows. It starts with you deciding what is the minimum amount that you can open up a new account with. What's the smallest sale your company will allow, right? That's where it starts. And then once you know what that is, you then work backwards. So let's say the minimum amount would be 1,000 shares. You could not start or whatever it did, 1,000 shares is a youth visit for any product, right? 10 of something, 50, whatever that minimum order, a dollar amount, whatever that is, that's your lowest. That's the last time you ask for the order, you want to end there. But when you ask for the order the first time, you want to ask for a significantly larger commitment on the first go-around. You get it? So then each time you ask for the order, first time is let's say, I'm making you get 10,000 shares. The second time you'd only ask for 5,000 shares. And with the next up, you'll ask for 2,500. And then the last time, then you'll step down to the minimum that your firm will allow for a new account and boom, that is called a step down sale. And let me just tell you something, step down sales are so incredibly effective. So if there's any way you can do that, now it doesn't work with a car, right? And really, I guess you could say, let's go start with a low model, but it's really not designed for something like that. For many, many products, it works incredibly well. So what are some of the patterns that you would use with a step down sale? Well, you would say to Bill then, you know, listen, all I'm asking for is 1% of your trust and I promise I'll earn the rest over time. I'll earn the other 99%. You get it? Because what you're saying is, dip your toe in the water, give me a little bit of trust. You'll see the results for yourself and then we can work bigger and better down the road. Here's another one. You know, Bill, you don't want to sell as small as this. After I split my commission with the firm and the government, I can't take my wife out for lunch. I'm not getting rich here. But what I do know is that once you see what a great job I do for you, this will serve as a benchmark for future business and we can work much bigger and better down the road. Sound fair enough? There's many, many different paths that will include more in the resource area in the exercise, right? Now, just to be clear, again, if you're selling for a product that doesn't allow for a step down, it doesn't change the fact that this is the point on the straight line, meaning when you're asked for the order of the second time, this is the point where many of your prospects are going to start to buy, especially the ones who have lower action thresholds. Because you've now cracked those first three numbers of the buying combination, which is pretty much enough to make most people low action thresholds closed. And while I really can't get you an exact number here, I'd say that on average, somewhere around 20% of the prospects who hit you with initial objection live closing right here as a result of you running that one simple loop. That's powerful stuff. The rest, however, are going to require a bit more persuading in the form of running additional loops.
